Minnesota more progressive states United States when comes marijuana policy, continues make positive adjustments cannabis laws.
state Minnesota still characterizes recreational marijuana possession misdemeanor felony, policies place decriminalize possession small amounts.
Those found possession 42.5 grams marijuana only punished fine $200.
Additionally, there conditional release policy place first time offenders, have offense removed from their record.
Offenders also required complete drug education course.
Those caught possession more than 42.5 grams marijuana face more serious penalties.
These crimes prosecuted felonies, which punished years more jail, fines $10,000 more.
Medical marijuana legalized Minnesota with passing Senate File 2470 2014.
Under law, Minnesota Commissioner Health tasked with regulating patient registry production distribution medical cannabis.
allows creation in-state manufacturers medical cannabis, each which opened four dispensaries.
There currently eight operational dispensaries Minnesota.
Home cultivation allowed.
licensed healthcare practitioner must certify qualified patients before they receive medical cannabis state.
terminal illness with life expectancy under year, where illness treatment leads following: Severe, intractable chronic pain, nausea severe vomiting, cachexia severe wasting.
July 2018, there 1,234 approved health care practitioners certify medical marijuana patients, 10,738 patients actively enrolled Minnesota's medical marijuana program.
Minnesota pass "The Industrial Hemp Development Act" 2015, which categorizes hemp "agricultural crop" empowers Commission Agriculture pilot program grow industrial hemp institutions higher education.
hemp designated grown research agricultural purposes only.
